title: Dortmanna colorata
intro: Dortmanna colorata, also known as Wild Strawberry or European Strawberry Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae) native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna colorata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and flowers are also used to make jams and preserves due to their sweet and slightly tart flavor.
